metric_value: Tools arriving and improving; Janus Web Server cited as key example
notes: Aberdeen finds web-enablement tools for mainframe were arriving at time of study

metric_value: Rewrite — write application from scratch on Web server
notes: Option 1 of 3 strategic choices identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: Migrate and upgrade — move mainframe app to web server then web-enable it
notes: Option 2 of 3 strategic choices identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: Upgrade in place — web-enable mainframe application while keeping it on mainframe
notes: Option 3 of 3 strategic choices — Aberdeen's recommended path

metric_value: Paradoxically positive — made it easier to keep and web-enable mainframe apps rather than replace them
notes: Aberdeen's central finding: Internet strengthened rather than weakened the mainframe case

metric_name: Scalability and robustness of rewritten apps
metric_value: Migration or rewriting results in applications that are less scalable and robust than original mainframe apps
notes: Key risk identified by Aberdeen for migration/rewrite strategies

metric_value: Product expected to improve and gain adoption for mainframe web-enablement
notes: Implicit Aberdeen endorsement of Janus Web Server as viable product category

metric_value: Acquired by Rocket Software in November 2012 for undisclosed sum; products continued under Rocket M204 brand
notes: Source: Rocket Software press release Nov 2012; StorageNewsletter coverage

metric_value: IBM mainframe (z/OS) remains in active production at major banks and government agencies nearly 30 years after study
notes: Aberdeen's prediction of mainframe viability proved fully correct

metric_value: Senior IS managers should reconsider rewriting or migrating mainframe apps; use new mainframe-software tools to web-enable in place
notes: Core prescriptive recommendation of the study

metric_value: In-place web-enablement offers cost-effective Internet connectivity with minimal impact on production environment
notes: Aberdeen characterizes in-place enablement as least risky and most cost-effective
